sourceMovie.title: Deep Sea
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Deep Sea (2023) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deep Sea clicked for you, Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Luck
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Deep Sea clicked for you, Luck (2022) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Deep Sea?
faq.answer: Try Raya and the Last Dragon, Luck, Wish Dragon—each shares DNA with Deep Sea while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Deep Sea?
faq.answer: Deep Sea (2023) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Drama, Fantasy, Mystery. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Deep Sea worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
